channel.title: Ad News Feed
channel.description: 広告プラットフォーム最新情報の自動収集フィード

item.title: [Google Ads] How I Use Claude with Google Ads
item.description:
Are you tired of expensive, rigid Google Ads tools that don’t actually understand your specific business? In this video, I’m pulling back the curtain on how I use Claude to optimize Google Ads campaigns faster and more effectively than manual checklists.
👉 Get Free Access to my "Keywords & SWOT Ana

item.title: [General] Unlock Keyword Gaps: AI SEO Strategy for Growth #shorts
item.description: Unlock your SEO potential by identifying crucial keyword gaps your competitors miss. Discover untapped opportunities using Google Keyword Planner and AI analysis for targeted growth. #KeywordResearch #SEOStrategy #DigitalMarketing #CompetitorAnalysis #SearchEngineOptimization

item.title: [Meta Ads] Social Proof Might Not Matter After All
item.description: Advertisers assume ads with thousands of comments and reactions perform better due to social proof, and fear making edits that would erase that engagement. But Jon duplicated an ad with 6,000 comments and the new version starting from scratch immediately matched and even beat the original's performa

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads on a Budget: $5-$20/Day Campaign Strategy #shorts
item.description: Maximize your impact with Google Ads on a tight budget. Focus on high-intent keywords like "tree service marketing agency." Start with exact match, then refine your strategy. Drive targeted clicks even with $5, $10, or $20 per day. #GoogleAds #SmallBusiness #DigitalMarketing #PPC #MarketingTips

item.title: [General] バナー制作のクオリティとスピードを両立する、「作り始める前」の準備術
item.description:
バナー制作って、意外と時間がかかりますよね。 私自身、1日を費やしてもバナー1点すら完成しない、という日が何度もありました。ある日ふと「自分は何に、こんなに時間を使っているんだろう？」と感じ、一度手を…

The post バナー制作のクオリティとスピードを両立する、「作り始める前」の準備術 first appeared on アナグラム株式会社.
item.category: アナグラム ブログ

item.title: [General] Site Links Feature Request
[General] Free SEO Tools List: 35 Free Tools for Keyword Research, Technical SEO, Backlinks, and Reporting
item.description: If you search for a free SEO tools list, most articles give you the same problem in a different format: a long set of names with very little context, no clear distinction between truly free tools and limited free tiers, and almost no guidance on which tools matter for which job. That approach is

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Keyword Planner: How to Find the Right Keywords, Estimate Search Demand, and Build a Smarter SEO and PPC Strategy
item.description: If you want better search visibility, stronger ad targeting, and a clearer view of what people actually look for in Google, learning how to use Google Keyword Planner is a practical place to start. It is one of the few keyword research tools built directly around Google Ads data, and that matters

item.title: [General] How to Use Hootsuite in 2026: Complete Guide to Setup, Scheduling, Analytics, Inbox, and Team Workflows
item.description: If you manage more than one social channel, you already know the real problem is not posting. The real problem is managing everything around posting. Content has to be planned, approved, published at the right time, adapted for each platform, monitored after it goes live, and measured in a way th

item.title: [General] Backlinks Explained: What They Are, How They Work, and How to Earn High-Quality Links for SEO
item.description: Backlinks are still one of the clearest signals that a page deserves attention. When another site links to your content, it creates more than a referral path. It creates context. It tells search engines what your page is about, how it relates to other content on the web, and whether people in you

item.title: [Meta Ads] Facebook Business Manager Explained: Setup, Permissions, Assets, Pixels, and Common Fixes
item.description: If you search for Facebook Business Manager, you will quickly notice something confusing: the term is still widely used, but Meta’s current interface and help documentation increasingly refer to Meta Business Suite, business portfolios, and related admin tools. That naming shift has created a gap

item.title: [General] Google Local Services Ads: Complete Guide to Setup, Cost, Ranking Factors, Reviews, and Better Leads
item.description: If you run a service business and want more calls, messages, and booked jobs from people who are already searching for help in your area, Google Local Services Ads deserve serious attention. They sit in one of the most valuable spots on the search results page. They are built for local intent. An

item.title: [General] Domain Authority Explained: How It’s Calculated, What a Good Score Looks Like, and How to Improve It
item.description: Domain authority is one of the most talked-about metrics in SEO, and also one of the most misunderstood. People check it before buying a domain, sizing up a competitor, pitching for backlinks, or reporting performance to a client. At the same time, many website owners treat it as if it were a dir

item.title: [General] How to Check Website Traffic: 12 Reliable Ways to Measure Your Site and Competitors
item.description: If you want better leads, better SEO decisions, and better marketing ROI, you need a clear answer to a basic question: how much traffic is your website actually getting, and where is that traffic coming from? A surprising number of teams still work from partial data. They look at a top-line sessi

item.title: [General] How to Conduct a Detailed Website Audit: The Complete 2026 Checklist for SEO, Speed, UX, Accessibility, and Conversions
item.description: A detailed website audit is the process of checking whether a site is technically sound, easy to use, easy to understand, easy to find, and able to turn visitors into business outcomes. Done properly, an audit does not stop at a list of issues. It explains what is wrong, why it matters, how serio

item.title: [General] Ultimate Guide to Using GoHighLevel in 2026: Setup, CRM, Automation, Pipelines, and Reporting
item.description: GoHighLevel is one of the few platforms that can sit at the center of lead capture, follow-up, sales pipeline management, appointment booking, messaging, and client reporting without forcing a business to stitch together a long list of separate tools. That is the main reason it keeps showing up i

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads for Nail Salons: Get More Clients! #shorts

Maximize your budget with smart bidding strategies. #GoogleAds #LocalSEO #LeadGeneration #SmallBusinessMarketing #NailSalonMarketing

item.title: [Meta Ads] Site Links Feature Request
item.description: This is a feature Meta desperately needs. Give advertisers the option of defining site links at an account level so that we don't need to keep removing Meta's suggested site links. It shouldn't be this difficult.

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads with $20/Day: Budget Tutorial Revealed #shorts
item.description: Can you really run Google Ads with just $20 per day? We break down when it's possible and when it's impossible to make a small budget work with Google Ads. #GoogleAds #SmallBudget #PPC #DigitalMarketing

item.title: [General] YouTube’s New TV-Style Channels Explained: What Coachella TV Means for Connected TV, Creators, and Brands
item.description: YouTube is testing a format that looks familiar to anyone who has spent time with traditional television or FAST streaming services: always-on, topic-based channels designed for lean-back viewing on connected TVs. The first high-profile example is Coachella TV, a 24/7 stream built around festival

item.title: [General] Ultimate Guide to Using Mailchimp for Email Marketing in 2026: Setup, Automation, Segmentation, Deliverability, and Reporting
item.description: Email marketing is still one of the highest-leverage channels a business can own, but the software alone does not create results. The platform matters, the setup matters, the list quality matters, and the way your campaigns are structured matters even more. Mailchimp remains one of the most widel

item.title: [General] Disney+ and Hulu Put TV Props on Facebook Marketplace: What “The Drama You Want” Reveals About Modern Streaming Marketing

Paid placements follow audiences around the internet. It works, but it is also predictable. That is why Disney+ and Hulu’s latest extension of “The Drama You Want” sta

item.title: [General] Instagram Now Lets Creators Schedule Trial Reels: How the Feature Works, When to Use It, and What It Means for Reach

item.title: [General] Emergency Service SEO: How to Capture “Near Me,” “Open Now,” and 24/7 Searches That Turn Into Calls
item.description: When someone searches for an emergency plumber, an after-hours electrician, a 24/7 locksmith, or a same-day HVAC repair company, they are not browsing. They are trying to solve a problem that is happening right now. A pipe is leaking. The AC is out in peak heat. A storefront door will not lock. A

item.title: [General] Neighborhood SEO for Real Estate: 15 Proven Ways to Win High-Intent Local Search Terms
item.description: Real estate search rarely starts with an agent’s name. It starts with place. A buyer looks up “best neighborhoods in Frisco for families.” A relocating seller searches “homes near downtown Naperville.” An investor types “cash flow rental neighborhoods in Tampa.” Even when the final conversion hap

item.title: [General] Law Firm SEO: The 90-Day Roadmap to Page One for Local, Practice Area, and AI Search Visibility
item.description: Law firm SEO is difficult for a simple reason: legal search is one of the most competitive, trust-sensitive environments on the internet. Prospective clients are not looking for entertainment. They are looking for answers that affect money, freedom, family, immigration status, business risk, or p

item.title: [General] How to Rank for “Near Me” Medical Searches in Competitive Markets: A Practical Local SEO Guide for Clinics, Groups, and Hospitals
item.description: When a patient searches for “urgent care near me,” “dermatologist near me,” “OBGYN near me,” or “walk-in clinic near me,” they are usually not browsing casually. They are trying to solve a real problem close to where they are, often on a phone, often with limited patience, and often with the inte

item.title: [General] E-commerce Schema Markup for Rich Results: Product, Merchant Listing, Variant, and Category Page Implementation Guide
item.description: Search engines do not buy products. People do. But search engines decide which product pages, category pages, store policies, and brand signals get understood well enough to be shown in enhanced search experiences. That is where e-commerce schema markup matters. For online stores, structured data

item.title: [General] 12 Best Marketing Conferences in May 2026: Dates, Locations, Costs, and Who Should Attend

It connected a luxury automaker, a contemporary artist with deep jazz influence, one of the most important names in modern

A company founded on April 1, 1976, and built through a series of category-defining products had a rare opportunity in 2026: mark a historic milestone without looking backward for its own sake. item.description: A truck carrying 413,793 KitKat bars vanished while traveling from central Italy to Poland. The shipment weighed roughly 12 metric tons, consisted of Formula 1-themed KitKat products, and disappeared at a time of year when confectionery demand is especially sensitive. Instead, it brought the launch into Brooklyn, where football, music, food, design, and community programming became the format for the unveiling itself. That choice

item.title: [General] Mr. Clean’s Biggest Magic Eraser Upgrade in 20 Years: What Changed, How It Means for Home Cleaning, and Which New Product Fits the Job
item.description: For a long time, the Mr. Clean Magic Eraser occupied a familiar place in American homes: part sponge, part problem-solver, something people reached for when a spray cleaner stalled out on scuffs, soap scum, or kitchen grime. That baseline familiarity is exactly why the brand’s latest product upda

item.title: [General] Coca-Cola’s “You Must Love Coke” Optical Illusion Campaign Explained: Why Red Appears in Blue Football Stadiums

On the surface, the premise is simple: place Coca-Cola in football environments where its signature red is unwelcome, and do it without actually using red. But

On the surface, that sounds like another technical infrastructure update. In practice, it matters because ad serving is the mechanism that decides which ad gets shown to which pers

Yet the best April Fools’ Day ads of 2026 show that these campaigns are not really about r

It introduced a new creative platform, new recurring characters, a new product tied to a social-media-driven drink trend, and

They celebrate more traffic, more impressions, more followers, or more email sends without proving wheth

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Analytics Dashboard Guide: How to Build a GA4 Dashboard for Traffic, Conversions, Revenue, and SEO

That sounds simple, but in practice it is where most teams get stuck. Google Analytics 4 gives you a large volume of event-based data, multiple rep

item.title: [General] Fanta x Xbox Limited-Edition Cans and Gaming Challenge: Flavors, Rewards, Release Dates, and How It Works
item.description: If you have seen the new Fanta x Xbox cans online and want the straight facts, here is the short version. item.title: [General] Evian’s “200 Years Young” Campaign Explained: How the Jeff Koons Collaboration Turns Alpine Heritage Into a Modern Brand Story
item.description: Anniversary campaigns often have a predictable problem. They are rich in dates, thin on meaning, and easy to forget once the press release cycle ends. item.title: [General] The Agentic Web Explained: How AI Agents Evaluate Websites, Content, and Commerce in 2026
item.description: For most of the commercial internet, the basic interaction pattern has been stable. A person opens a browser, runs a search, scans results, clicks pages, compares options, and decides what to do next. Websites have been built around that behavior. item.title: [Google Ads] Master Google Ads Call Conversions: Track Every Click! #shorts

item.title: [Google Ads] DashThis Tutorial – Build Google Analytics & Google Ads Dashboards Fast
item.description: DashThis makes it easy to create professional marketing dashboards without spending hours building reports from scratch. In this tutorial, I'll show you how to connect Google Analytics and Google Ads to DashThis and walk you through the steps to create dashboards that give you actionable insights.

item.title: [Google Ads] Why LSA and Google Ads Cannibalize Each Other (And How to Fix It)

item.title: [General] Walmart and Vizio’s CTV Strategy: How Unified Logins, Shoppable Placements, and Closed-Loop Measurement Connect TV Ads to Retail Sales
item.description: For years, connected TV promised better targeting than linear television, better storytelling than standard display, and better household-level reach than many digital channels. item.title: [General] March Madness Sports Betting in 2026: What $3.3 Billion in Wagers Means for Sportsbooks, Advertisers, and Fan Retention

item.title: [General] WCAG 2.2 in 2026: Enterprise Web Accessibility Requirements, Checklist, and Implementation Plan
item.description: If your organization still treats accessibility as a final QA step, 2026 is the year that approach becomes expensive. item.title: [General] LinkedIn Live Will Require Scheduled Events: What the End of Spontaneous Livestreams Means for Marketers
item.description: LinkedIn is changing how live video works on the platform, and the practical takeaway is simple: spontaneous livestreaming is going away. item.title: [General] Etsy’s Brand Refresh Explained: What SYLVAIN Changed in the Visual Identity and Why It Matters for Ecommerce Discovery
item.description: When Etsy updates its brand, the change matters for more than design-watchers.
